redGreenRefactorEvidence.refactor: scrub leaves under denylist object keys to preserve shape

redGreenRefactorEvidence.green: 016 migration, WebhookRawEvent, repo, DatabaseRawWebhookEventRecorder, handle() 5 exits, build_webhook_service wiring

implementationSummary: Persist every inbound TikTok webhook delivery to webhook_raw_events with denylist PII redaction and fail-safe recording that never blocks the 3s ACK.
assumptions: ADR-012 interim in-DB raw archive is the correct storage choice for Phase 2
Recording 401/400 paths is required for #382 type-string collection despite unauthenticated write surface
risks: Unauthenticated storage amplification on 401/400 without rate limits (security-review medium) — mitigate with nginx rate limit + retention follow-up
PII denylist is best-effort; extend if #382 surfaces new field names

green: 31 unit tests (redaction + raw log + existing webhook suite)
refactor: MODULE.md + webhooks.md Raw audit log section
migration test for 015 updated to downgrade to 014_action_cards

acceptanceCriteriaMet: Every inbound TikTok webhook (incl. 401/400) persists redacted row with correct http_status / processing_status
Recorder failure does not change HTTP response
Docs updated; catalog table / Confirmed column untouched
notes: Security review: 1 medium (unauth write amplification). Follow-ups: rate limit, retention pruning, S3 Phase 3, denylist extensions from #382.
